articles.title: Severe Heatwave Hits Uttar Pradesh, Banda Records 45.6°C - India News Network
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text: Banda, located in Uttar Pradesh's Bundelkhand region, recorded an exceptionally high temperature of 45.6 degrees Celsius on Tuesday, 28 April 2026. This increase in temperature is part of a severe heatwave currently affecting several areas of the state, as reported by the India Meteorological Department (IMD). Other regions in Uttar Pradesh also reported elevated temperatures. Orai reached 43.8 degrees Celsius, while Jhansi recorded 42.4 degrees Celsius. The towns of Fatehgarh and Hamirpur had temperatures of 42.2 degrees Celsius each, and Agra experienced 42.1 degrees Celsius. As communities contend with the intense heat, locals are taking measures to cope with the rising temperatures. Many have sought refuge in shaded areas or frequented cooler places to avoid the midday sun. "The heatwave conditions are expected to last, and we advise residents to stay hydrated and keep cool as much as possible," an official from the IMD stated. The heatwave is indicative of the broader climatic trends affecting the region, especially in the summer months. The IMD has urged the public to exercise caution during this period, advising against outdoor activities during peak heat hours. Further analysis from the department suggests that climatic conditions may continue to create challenges for residents in Uttar Pradesh, particularly in the Bundelkhand region, known for its vulnerability to extreme weather. As people in Uttar Pradesh cope with this severe weather, nearby regions are also experiencing fluctuations in their weather patterns. The Indian Meteorological Department continues to monitor the situation closely, providing updates as conditions evolve. Weather forecasts indicate that while this heatwave is significant, sporadic rainfall could also emerge, offering some reprieve in the coming days. Given the impact on daily activities and health, authorities are emphasising preventative measures for communities across Uttar Pradesh to mitigate the effects of such extreme heat conditions.

articles.title: Scorching Heat Grips Uttar Pradesh, Hazy Skies and Thunderstorm Predicted Today - The News Agency
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Lucknow, April 30 (TNA) Uttar Pradesh is sweltering under intense summer heat today, with hazy skies blanketing the state and temperatures soaring between 32-39°C across major cities, as reported by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) and AccuWeather. In the capital Lucknow, early-morning readings clocked 32.4°C, with southeasterly winds at 7.4 km/h and 54% humidity.
In comparison, hotter interiors like Fursatganj hit 39.2°C and Prayagraj reached 34.6°C, prompting heat advisories for vulnerable groups. Forecasters predict daytime highs of 33-36°C in Lucknow and nearby areas, with isolated western districts like Agra potentially exceeding 40°C under partly cloudy conditions and minimal rainfall under 2 mm statewide.
Late afternoon thunderstorms or light showers could bring brief relief in pockets, accompanied by gusty winds up to 30 km/h, though air quality remains unhealthy due to dust haze and pollutants, urging residents to limit outdoor exposure.
Regional variations highlight eastern UP’s slightly cooler zones around Varanasi at 34°C with rising winds, contrasting dry heat in the west, as no major western disturbances influence the pattern per IMD’s extended outlook. Nighttime lows are expected to settle at 24-26°C, but escalating mercury through early May calls for hydration, midday sun avoidance, and crop protection for farmers amid persistent dry spells.
